priorBlockingIssueAssessment.issue: Python normalization wrongly required explicit prompt/completion before normalizing from total_tokens.
priorBlockingIssueAssessment.evidence: CursorConnectorManager.swift Python normalize_usage now normalizes on `if normalized_total > 0:` with no `has_explicit_primary` gate (line 1139 in current file; commit fdadb8f diff).

priorBlockingIssueAssessment.issue: Python fallback could run despite explicit total/cache buckets.
priorBlockingIssueAssessment.evidence: Fallback is now in the `elif` branch after `if normalized_total > 0` (line 1158), and `normalized_total` is computed from `max(total, prompt+completion+cache_creation+cache_read)` (lines 1133-1134), so explicit total/cache buckets prevent fallback.

blockingIssues.description: The Python proxy fallback gate no longer requires all explicit exact buckets to be absent. Because `explicit_total` excludes `reasoning_tokens` (line 1133), a reasoning-only exact payload can leave `normalized_total == 0` and still enter fallback via `elif prompt_char_estimate + output_char_estimate > 0`, causing heuristic prompt/completion estimation despite explicit exact usage.
blockingIssues.evidence: Validation contract VAL-TOKEN-004 requires fallback only when input/output/cacheRead/cacheCreation/reasoning are all unavailable (validation-contract.md line 19). Swift path still enforces this (`else if ... cacheCreation == 0 && cacheRead == 0 && reasoningTokens == 0` at line 780), but Python path does not.

codeReview.summary: The fix resolves both previously reported blockers (total-only normalization and total/cache fallback gating), but introduces a new blocking regression in the Python proxy: fallback can execute when exact reasoning tokens are present.

issues.description: Python fallback branch does not guard on `reasoning_tokens == 0`, violating the exact-buckets fallback gate for VAL-TOKEN-004 and diverging from the Swift normalizeUsageEvent behavior.
issues.evidence: Python lines 1133-1134 + 1158 vs Swift line 780 in the same file.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The token-accounting-worker skill requires TDD red/green with failing scoped tests first, but both the original failed feature and this fix report `tests.added: []` while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` remains true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff files for d987f786 and 87eb822c both list no added tests; commits b88f171 and fdadb8f modify implementation only; SKILL.md step 3 requires adding/updating failing scoped tests first and step 7 requires truthful deviation reporting.

summary: Re-run review failed: prior blocking issues were fixed, but the Python normalize_usage fallback gate still violates VAL-TOKEN-004 for reasoning-token-only exact payloads, so the fix is not fully complete.
